title: Daily Briefing — Mar 30
headline: Extreme Fear and crisis-mode VIX are flashing opportunity—so why is everyone still selling?
body: Right, so the big picture: VIX is ripping at 30.91, well above the SMA20 (25.84). That’s a crisis regime and volatility expansion—classic risk-off. SPY is down -1.7% at $634.09 and has closed below the daily 20 MA ($664.71), which triggers the technical exit rule (R006). But here’s the kicker: Fear & Greed is at 10.4. That’s deep, deep Extreme Fear territory (R013). When the crowd’s panicking, we start watching for mean reversion.

Sector rotation is clear: Energy (XLE $62.56), Materials (XLB $48.91), Industrials (XLI $159.20), Staples (XLP $81.78), Utilities (XLU $45.59), and Real Estate (XLRE $40.01) are all leading, and risk levels are LOW. Tech and Financials are lagging hard—XLK and XLF both in HIGH risk, with Financials acting as the canary (R012) after a -2.5% dump to $47.81, approaching key SMA200 ($52.54). It’s classic defensive rotation.

Contrarian lens: With Extreme Fear and a VIX spike, historical analogs like March 2020, October 2022, and even late 2015/2016 all led to monster mean reversion rallies once the selling got exhausted. Breadth is ugly, tech is oversold (AAPL $248.80 vs SMA200 $247.70, MSFT $356.77 vs SMA200 $477.38), but if you chase shorts here, you’re playing chicken with the mean reversion zone. Abundance mindset—next trade, not last trade.
callout_body:
SPY: $634.09 (daily close below 20 MA $664.71) — technical exit
VIX: 30.91 (crisis regime)
XLK: $129.92 (approaching SMA200 $138.02) — wait for stabilization
XLE: $62.56 (leading, above all moving averages)
XLF: $47.81 (approaching SMA200 $52.54) — canary, watch for further breakdown
Extreme Fear: 10.4 (mean reversion watch triggered)
action_items: Reduce risk in lagging sectors (Tech, Financials)—no fresh longs until daily closes reclaim 20 MA.
Watch XLE, XLU, XLP and select energy/materials tickers for rotational plays, but don’t chase green—wait for pullback entries.
closing_quote: Guys, if it’s in the press, it’s in the price. Panic is the setup, not the confirmation.
